gpt55_review_summary: APPROVED by GPT-5.5/high reviewer Godel with no findings; reviewer checked strict release-safe validation, custom extension wheel wording, stale adapter-wheel assumptions, and checked-in/package template sync.
verification: fresh venv wheel smoke imported cachet.adapters.vllm, cachet.adapters.sglang, vllm_kv_injection, sglang_kv_injection, and read the packaged engine-probe template -> wheel smoke ok
GitHub CI Test and build for PR #304 -> success
what_changed: Databricks engine-probe tests now prove the provider-backed vLLM path installs only the vLLM runtime package plus the Cachet wheel, with adapter imports supplied by the vendored modules in that wheel.
Stale standalone vllm_kv_injection/sglang_kv_injection wheel examples were renamed to custom extension wheel fixtures while preserving --extra-wheel-uri as an explicit extension hook.
Engine-probe CLI help, README guidance, and checked-in/package Databricks bundle templates now document that Cachet ships the built-in vLLM/SGLang adapter modules.
why: After vendoring engine adapters into Cachet, Databricks release probes should not require separate adapter wheels before the native connector wiring is tested on AWS g6/L4.
